popularStory.headline:
Helen Warren Foote
(about 1529 – 18 July 1558)
popularStory.description:
Helen Warren married John Foote of Royston England. 

They had 4 children: Alice, Elizabeth, Robert, and John.

(https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/7646414/helen-foote)

sourceList.title: Helen Warren Foote, "Find A Grave Index"  is wrong, the widow Ellen Foote married John Hall

zh.lifeSummary: 當Helen Warren alias Waller於大約1529年出生在英國坎布利其爾巴辛波時，她的父親Richard Warren alias Waller30歲，母親Alice Jenawaye25歲。她於大約1548年與John Foote在英國賀特福爾羅伊斯頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1559年1月19日之後在英國倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Helen Warren alias Waller于大约1529年出生在英国坎布利其爾巴辛波时，她的父亲Richard Warren alias Waller30岁，母亲Alice Jenawaye25岁。她于大约1548年与John Foote在英国賀特福爾羅伊斯頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1559年1月19日之后在英国倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Waren
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Warren  .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: shortened form of   Warren  .
occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.
English:

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: alias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Jorge, Jesus, Arturo, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Miguel.
Spanish: from the medieval personal name Arias, of disputed origin.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

nameMeanings.name: Waller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English waler, waliere, walour, waller ‘builder of walls, mason’.
English: in Sussex, perhaps a topographic name for someone who lived by a prominent wall.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English waller, a derivative of Middle English walle, Old English wælle, wælle. It is a West Midlands dialect form of Weller .

variants.name: Wallar
meanings: English: variant of   Waller  .

variants.name: Weller
meanings: English (Surrey, Sussex, and Kent): topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English weller, willer, an agent derivative of Middle English welle (in southern England) and wille (in south western England), from Old English wella, well(e), wiella, wielle, willa, wyll(a), wylle. This type of name, a topographic term + -er, is particularly typical of Sussex and its neigbouring counties.
English: occupational name for someone who casts metal or who boils salt, from Middle English weller, willer, a derivative with -er of Old English wellan, wiellan ‘to boil’. Compare 5 below.
North German: variant of   Welle  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Helen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name (Greek Hēlēnē) borne in classical legend by a famous beauty, wife of Menelaus, whose seizure by the Trojan prince Paris sparked off the Trojan War. Her name is of uncertain origin; it may be connected with a word meaning ‘ray’ or ‘sunbeam’ compare Greek hēlios ‘sun’. It has sometimes been taken as connected with the Greek word meaning ‘Greek’, Hellēn, but this is doubtful. In the early Christian period the name was borne by the mother of the Emperor Constantine, who is now usually known by the Latin version of her name, Helena. She is credited with having found the True Cross in Jerusalem. She was born in about 248 , probably in Bithynia. However, in medieval England it was believed that she had been born in Britain, which greatly increased the popularity of the name there.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Helen Warren alias Waller was born about 1529, in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England, her father, Richard Warren alias Waller, was 30 and her mother, Alice Jenawaye, was 25. She married John Foote about 1548, in Royston, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 19 January 1559, in London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Helen Warren alias Waller est née environ 1529, à Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Warren alias Waller, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Alice Jenawaye, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé John Foote environ 1548, à Royston, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 19 janvier 1559, à Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Helen Warren alias Waller um 1529 in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Warren alias Waller 30 und ihre Mutter Alice Jenawaye 25. Sie hat um 1548 in Royston, Hertfordshire, England John Foote geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 19. Januar 1559 in London, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Helen Warren alias Waller è nata il circa 1529 nel Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England, sua padre, Richard Warren alias Waller, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Alice Jenawaye, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato John Foote circa 1548, nel Royston, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 19 gennaio 1559, nel London, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Helen Warren alias Waller(이)가 대략 1529년 영국 캄브리지실르 배징보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Warren alias Waller(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Jenawaye(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1548년 영국 허트퍼드실르 로이스턴에서 John Foote(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1559년 1월 19일 이후 영국 런던에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Helen Warren alias Waller nasceu aproximadamente 1529, em Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Warren alias Waller, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Alice Jenawaye, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com John Foote aproximadamente 1548, em Royston, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 19 de janeiro de 1559, em London, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Helen Warren alias Waller родилась около 1529 г. в местности бассингбурн, камбриджешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Warren alias Waller было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Jenawaye, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Foote около 1548 г. в местности роистон, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 19 январь 1559 г. в местности Лондон, Англия.
